content.en: Who’s a good dog?! Dogs are great! When we come home, they show us love and happiness! They make us laugh. They make us walk! We play with them.
content.tw: 誰是乖狗狗？！狗狗真棒！回到家的時候，狗狗會用愛與快樂讓我們開懷大笑！我們和狗狗出門散步、玩遊戲，度過快樂的時光。

content.en: Dogs want to be with others. They want to do everything together. That is why they look so happy to see us!
content.tw: 狗是群居動物，喜歡跟別人待在一起，也喜歡一起做各種事情。所以每次看到我們回家的時候，牠們總是特別興奮！

content.en: When we go out, we worry. Our dogs do not want to be alone. But we cannot be with them all the time…
content.tw: 不過，當我們出門的時候，也會擔心家裡的狗狗。牠們不喜歡孤單，但我們又不可能隨時陪在牠們的身邊……

content.en: What can we do?! We can go online and find... TV for dogs! TV for dogs are videos made for dogs.
content.tw: 那該怎麼辦呢？其實，我們可以上網找「狗狗電視」！這是專門為狗狗設計的影片。

content.en: Our dogs can watch other dogs, other animals, fun toys, and much more! They can watch them in blue and yellow. Those are the colors dogs see best.
content.tw: 牠們可以看其他的狗、各種動物、有趣的玩具等等。影片中的畫面也會以藍色跟黃色為主，因為這是狗狗最容易看清楚的顏色。

content.en: Thunder, fireworks, and other loud sounds can scare our dogs. TV for dogs does not scare dogs with sounds. Our dogs can listen to calm music and nice sounds.
content.tw: 打雷、放煙火或其他巨大聲響，常常會讓狗狗感到害怕。但狗狗電視不會播放會嚇到牠們的聲音。相反地，裡面有舒緩的音樂跟輕鬆的自然聲響。

content.en: Now we do not have to worry. We can go to school or work. Our dogs will not feel alone!
content.tw: 現在我們不用擔心了。我們可以去上學或工作。我們的狗狗將不會感到孤獨！

vocabulary.preface: 狗是群居動物，不喜歡孤獨。現在有新的辦法陪伴狗狗。

content.text:
1. music 音樂。
Hey, are those your headphones Buddy is wearing? 巴弟戴著你的耳機嗎?
Yeah, Buddy likes soft music. 是的，巴弟喜歡輕柔的音樂。
Dogs like music? That’s news to me. 狗狗喜歡音樂?這可是新聞。

2. sound 聲音。
They really do. 牠們真的喜歡。
They’re scared of loud and noisy sounds. 牠們害怕吵鬧的聲音。
But why the headphones? 但是為什麼戴耳機?
Oh, I don’t want music when I’m watching TV. 我看電視的時候不要有音樂。

3. listen 注意聽。
So you watch TV and Buddy listens to music. 所以你看電視，巴弟聽音樂。

4. all the time 整天。
I can’t be with Buddy all the time. 我不能整天跟牠在一起。
Music makes him feel less alone. 音樂讓牠比較不孤單。

vocabulary.postscript:
你記得今天的單字嗎?
music音樂 sound聲音 listen注意聽 all the time整天

quiz.question: 1. Where can we find TV for dogs?
2. What kind of sounds does TV for dogs have?
3. What colors can dogs see best?

title: A Long, Crazy Drive to South Africa

content.en: Do you like to go for long drives? How long is your longest drive? Two men wanted to go for a very long drive. They wanted to start in the U.K. They wanted to drive to South Africa! How crazy!
content.tw: 你喜歡開車長途旅行嗎？有兩名男子有個瘋狂的想法。他們想從英國出發，一路開車到南非！

content.en: The men got a small car. It has three wheels. One is in the front. Two are in the back. The car is 25 years old!
content.tw: 他們找來一輛三輪小汽車。這輛老車有已經有二十五年車齡了！

content.en: This old car is not strong. But the men drove it in wet jungles. They drove it in hot, dry deserts. They drove it up and down mountains! That is crazy!
content.tw: 這兩人開著老車穿越潮濕的叢林與乾燥的沙漠。老車翻山越嶺，太不可思議了！

content.en: The car broke down many times. But people in Africa fixed it every time! How lucky!
content.tw: 這台車在路上故障了很多次。但幸運的是，總會有人能幫他們修好！

content.en: The men drove and drove. They drove in 22 countries! They saw elephants! Giraffes ran next to their car!
content.tw: 這兩名男子開車穿越了二十二個國家！他們還看見了大象跟長頸鹿在車旁奔跑！

content.en: Sometimes there was danger. A bus almost hit them. And in some countries, there were wars. Whoa, how scary…
content.tw: 在這趟旅程中，他們差一點與巴士擦撞，還經過深陷戰亂的國家，十分危險。

content.en: The men drove 22,000 kilometers! That is the longest drive for a car with three wheels.
content.tw: 他們總共行駛了二萬二千公里，創下了三輪汽車最長行駛距離的新紀錄！

content.en: What a crazy drive! Where will these two men drive next?
content.tw: 這是一段多麼瘋狂的旅程！這兩位男子接下來會開往哪裡呢？

vocabulary.preface: 這兩名男士開著三個輪子的汽車，花了一百多天穿越非洲。

content.text:
1. longest 最長的。
How long was your longest ride in a car? 你坐過最長的一趟車程有多長?
Hmmm, about 700 kilometers from Ulaanbaatar to Gobi. 大約七百公里，從烏蘭巴托到戈壁。

2. country 國家。
In Mongolia? 在蒙古?
Yeah. It’s a big country. 對，它是個很大的國家。

3. desert 沙漠。
Did you see deserts and sand dunes? 有看到沙漠跟沙丘嗎?
Oh yes. The scenery was amazing! 有，很精采的風景!

4. break down 拋錨。
Lucky you! 你很幸運! I was once stuck on a bus in Canada. 我有一次被卡在加拿大的巴士上。
Oh no. 真糟糕。
It broke down after 5 hours. 它開了五個小時，然後拋錨了。

vocabulary.postscript:
你喜歡坐車嗎? 來讀單字。
longest最長的 country國家 desert沙漠 break down拋錨

quiz.question: 1. Where did the men start the drive?
2. How many wheels does the men's car have?
3. What animals ran next to their car?

title: A Little Dark Chocolate Might Be Good for You

content.en: Did you know our bodies can be younger or older than we are? Maybe you are ten, but your body is twelve!
content.tw: 你可知道，我們身體的年齡可能會比實際年齡年輕或者年老呢?

content.en: Our bodies' age shows how worn out our cells are. How do scientists know they are wearing out?
content.tw: 身體年齡從細胞衰退的程度可以看出來。那麼科學家怎麼知道細胞正在衰退呢？

content.en: Each cell has chromosomes. A chromosome looks like an "X." It has four ends. There is a cap on each end. The four caps keep the chromosome safe.
content.tw: 每個細胞裡都有染色體。染色體看起來像個英文字母「X」，一共有四個末端。每個末端都有一個用來保護染色體的小帽子。

content.en: But as a cell gets worn out, the caps get shorter. That is how scientists know.
content.tw: 隨著細胞衰退或是變弱，保護帽會越變越短。科學家用這個來做判斷。

content.en: But what can stop the caps from getting shorter? Can dark chocolate help? Some scientists are studying it!
content.tw: 現在，有些科學家正在研究，怎麼樣讓保護帽變短的過程慢下來。

content.en: The scientists found that people who eat dark chocolate have longer caps than people who do not eat it. The longer caps mean their chromosomes look younger!
content.tw: 研究發現，常吃黑巧克力的人，保護帽比不吃的人長！保護帽越長，代表染色體越年輕！

content.en: But wait! We cannot eat all the dark chocolate we want! It has sugar and fat too. So scientists say a little dark chocolate with lots of cocoa is okay.
content.tw: 但是不能開懷大吃黑巧克力喔！它含有糖跟脂肪，所以科學家建議我們吃一點就好。

content.en: Scientists are learning more. But for now, a little dark chocolate might be good for your body! Yum!
content.tw: 科學家正在了解更多。但目前來說，吃一點黑巧克力可能對你的身體有益！真好吃！

vocabulary.preface: 科學家正在研究，如何讓人體老化的速度變慢。

content.text:
1. younger 比較年輕。
Look at this photo of me when I was younger. 你看我比較年輕時的照片。Wow, you look so different! 哇，你看起來很不一樣!

2. longer 比較長。
How so? 怎麼說?
Your hair was longer, 頭髮比較長，and you looked like a teenager! 而且看起來才十幾歲!
Come on, I wasn’t that young! 好啦，沒那麼年輕!

3. wear out 變弱，或是衰退。
Hey, you don’t look old now. 你現在看起來也並不老!
But I feel worn out all the time. 但是我經常覺得好累。
You just need a vacation. 你只是需要去度假。

4. body 身體。
Definitely! It’ll be good for my body and soul. 一定要的! 對我的身體和靈魂都有好處!

vocabulary.postscript: 今天你學到哪些單字? younger比較年輕 longer比較長 wear out變弱，衰退 body身體

quiz.question: 1. How many caps does one chromosome have?
2. What happens when a cell gets worn out?
3. What did the scientists study?

title: Rice Seeds Feel the Sound of Rain

content.en: What makes seeds grow into plants?
content.tw: 是什麼讓種子長成一棵一棵植物？

content.en: We put seeds in water or the ground. We do this when the weather is good for them. We water seeds in the ground. Then we wait. The seeds start to grow.
content.tw: 天氣好的時候播種，把種子放進土裡或水中，並且替土裡的種子澆水。然後就等它發芽。

content.en: Two scientists thought sounds might make seeds grow too. Then, they found the sound of raindrops makes rice seeds grow!
content.tw: 有兩位科學家發現，雨滴落下的聲音似乎能讓稻米種子生長變快！

content.en: But, how does the sound of raindrops do this? Seeds do not have ears!
content.tw: 但這是怎麼回事？種子又沒有耳朵！

content.en: A raindrop can shake the water and the ground. The seeds feel the shaking. It moves very small parts in the seeds. The seeds start to grow. And, they grow faster than seeds that did not feel any shaking from raindrops!
content.tw: 原來雨滴落到地面上會產生震動，種子感覺到這種震動就開始發芽，而且比沒有感受到震動的種子長得快！

content.en: How much faster do the seeds grow? Scientists tried raindrops for light, medium, and heavy rain. With raindrops for medium and heavy rain, the seeds grow 30 to 40 percent faster.
content.tw: 科學家模擬了小雨、中雨跟大雨三種情況。他們發現，在中雨跟大雨的震動下，種子的生長速度可以提高百分之三十到四十！

content.en: Scientists want to study other natural sounds. Maybe seeds can feel the sound of the wind!
content.tw: 現在，科學家還想研究其他的大自然的聲音。也許種子也能感受到風的聲音喔！

content.en: Can you think of other natural sounds? Maybe you will find one that makes seeds grow faster too!
content.tw: 你能想到其他大自然的聲音嗎？也許你也會發現一個能讓種子長得更快的聲音！

vocabulary.preface: 種子可以感覺到土地的震動，而調整它的發芽生長。

content.text:
1. sound 聲音。
Is that the sound of raindrops? 那是下雨的聲音嗎?
Yeah, it’s raining… again! 對，又下雨了!
But we had sunshine this morning! 但是早上還有太陽的!

2. plant 植物。
Think of it this way. 你可以這麼想。 It’s good for the plants. 下雨對植物好。 But not good for me! 但是對我不好。

3. faster 快一點。
Well, you want your tomatoes to grow faster, don’t you? 你不是想要你的番茄長快一點?
Yeah, true. I can’t wait to use them for my pasta. 真的，我等不及做義大利麵要用了。

4. weather 天氣。
Then you should love this weather. 那你應該喜歡這天氣。
Yeah, for my dinner. 好吧，為了我的晚餐。

vocabulary.postscript: 你有種過什麼植物嗎?來讀單字。sound聲音 plant植物 faster快一點 weather天氣

quiz.question: 1. What seeds did the scientists study?
2. What made the seeds grow faster?
3. How much faster did the seeds grow?

title: Taiwan has World’s Best Latte Art Maker

content.en: Some people can make pretty pictures... in coffee! They make them with very, very small milk bubbles. The pictures are called latte art.
content.tw: 有些人可以在咖啡的表面，用很小的牛奶泡泡畫出各種圖案，這叫做咖啡拉花。

content.en: A man from Taiwan started making latte art 12 years ago. He started competing in latte art contests eight years ago.
content.tw: 一名臺灣男子學習咖啡拉花已經十二年。八年前，他開始參加比賽。

content.en: This year, he wanted to compete in a big contest in the U.S. Before the contest, he trained for four months. Every day, he made 100 drinks with latte art! About 100 people helped him with money, training, and many other things!
content.tw: 今年，他想參加在美國舉行的大型比賽。為了這場比賽他訓練了四個月，每天做一百次拉花！一百多人提供資金，支持他訓練。

content.en: The man went to the contest. Thirty-three people from around the world competed. They made a lot of latte art very fast!
content.tw: 然後他前往美國，跟來自世界各地的選手較量，他們必須在短時間做出許多拉花作品。

content.en: At the end, the man made six drinks in 10 minutes! He made pictures of a raccoon, a giraffe, and red pandas.
content.tw: 這名男子在十分鐘內完成了六杯咖啡拉花！他在飲料上畫出浣熊、長頸鹿跟小貓熊。

content.en: And, the man won the contest! Now Taiwan has the best maker of latte art in the world!
content.tw: 結果他贏了比賽！現在，臺灣擁有世界第一的咖啡拉花大師！

content.en: This man says you should do what you love to do. He says great things can happen when you do that!
content.tw: 他說只要全心投入自己熱愛的事，就有機會創造出偉大的成就！

content.en: What do you love to do?
content.tw: 你熱愛做什麼呢？

vocabulary.preface: 咖啡拉花也有世界冠軍，今年是由台灣選手獲得!

content.text:
1. art 藝術。
Wow, my coffee has a rose on it! 哇，我的咖啡上面有一朵玫瑰。
Mine has a cat! 我的有一隻貓!
Our coffee lady makes perfect latte art! 我們的咖啡女士會做完美的拉花。

2. do 做。
I’ve tried to do this at home. 我在家裡試著做。 It’s hard! 好難喔!
I know. It takes time to learn the trick. 我知道，要花不少時間才能學到訣竅。

3. pretty 美麗的。
It’s worth it. 很值得。Just look at the pretty picture! 看看這個漂亮的圖案!

4. contest 比賽。
Did you know that she joined latte art contests? 你知道她參加過拉花比賽嗎? Our coffee lady? 我們的咖啡女士? Did she win? 她有沒有贏?
Yep, more than once. 贏了，還不止一次。

vocabulary.postscript: 你想學學看這項手藝嗎? art藝術 do做 pretty美麗的 contest比賽

quiz.question: 1. Where was the contest?
2. How did the man train every day?
options: a. He made 100 drinks with latte art.
c. He drank six drinks in ten minutes.

quiz.question: 3. What animal did the man make with latte art?

title: Man Runs to Help Scientists and Blind People

content.en: Narrator: Scientists around the world worry about blindness. When people cannot see, their lives are not easy. So one blind man ran a marathon. That is more than 42 kilometers! People gave him money for running. He helps blind people and scientists with the money.
content.tw: 當人們看不見的時候，生活會變得很困難。一位失明的男子決定挑戰馬拉松賽！許多人捐款給他，而他用這個錢來支持視障人士和科學研究。

content.en: How did the man run safely? He wore smart glasses! He could not see, but the glasses have a camera. The camera works with an app.
content.tw: 但是他要怎麼安全地完成比賽？他戴上一付智慧眼鏡，眼鏡上裝了攝影機。

content.en: Other people had the app too. They saw the marathon from the man's smart glasses! These people were his eyes! The people talked to the man. They told him where to go. Person A: “Turn right!” And they cheered him. Person B: “You can do it!”
content.tw: 其他人下載他的智慧眼鏡應用程式，就可以用那付眼鏡看馬拉松賽，參賽的盲眼男子則是用聽的。他們一路告訴男子該往右還是往左，也不斷替他加油。

content.en: Narrator: The man finished the marathon! He raised one hundred fifteen thousand Taiwan dollars! It will help blind people have better lives. It will help scientists find ways to stop blindness.
content.tw: 結果男子順利跑完馬拉松，還獲得十一萬五千元台幣捐款！他將支持科學家研究預防失明的方法，幫助視障人士過更好的生活。

content.en: Scientists in Taiwan worry about blindness too. Most young Taiwanese cannot see well. Doctors say being outside for two hours a day helps kids see better.
content.tw: 科學家擔心很多臺灣年輕人都有近視，醫生說，每天到戶外活動兩個小時，有助於保護視力。

content.en: Go outside! Your eyes need it!
content.tw: 到戶外走走吧！你的眼睛需要它！

vocabulary.preface: 這位雙眼失明的男子，不但跑完馬拉松，還能募款。

content.text:
1. glasses 眼鏡。
Are you really running the Taipei Marathon this weekend? 你這周末真的要去跑台北馬拉松?
I am! I can’t wait! 對，我等不及了!
But you wear glasses. 但是你戴眼鏡。
That won’t be a problem. 那不是問題。

2. blind 眼盲的。
What if you lose them? 要是眼鏡掉了怎麼辦? You’ll be blind! 你會變成盲人!
Not blind, just a little blurry. 不會是盲人，只是看東西有點模糊。

3. worry 擔憂。
Actually I’m more worried about my legs than my eyes. 其實我更擔心我的腿，而不是眼睛。

4. cheer 加油。
I’ll be there to cheer you on. 我會去替你加油。
Thank you! 謝謝! Just don’t expect me to win. 只是別期待我會贏。

vocabulary.postscript: 我們一起來讀單字。glasses眼鏡 blind眼盲的 worry擔憂 cheer加油

quiz.question: 1. How long is a marathon?
2. What did the man wear to run safely?
3. What do doctors say helps kids see better?

title: Do These Chimps Want New Jobs?

content.en: Do These Chimps Want New Jobs? One day at a zoo in Kaohsiung, chimpanzees had newspapers and books! The chimps put them close to their faces. They looked and looked at them.
content.tw: 有一天，高雄的某一座動物園裡有黑猩猩在讀報紙和書！

content.en: One chimp had a picture book. One took a newspaper from another chimp. And one looked at job ads in a newspaper!
content.tw: 一隻黑猩猩手上拿著故事書，有兩隻猩猩在搶報紙，還有一隻在看報紙上的求職廣告！

content.en: Kids were visiting the zoo. They laughed at this chimp's newspaper. Job ads tell people where to find work. Maybe the chimp did not want to be at the zoo. Maybe it wanted a new job!
content.tw: 到動物園參觀的小朋友 看到黑猩猩看求職廣告都笑了。也許牠不愛動物園，想找新工作！

content.en: Were the chimps reading? No! Chimps cannot read! If they could read, maybe all of the zoo's chimps would find new jobs!
content.tw: 當然，黑猩猩不識字，也不會真的看報紙！

content.en: The zookeepers gave the chimps these newspapers and books. They made them with cardboard and newspapers. They cut small holes in the cardboard. Then they put sunflower seeds in the holes! Ooohh!
content.tw: 其實，這些報紙和書本是動物園管理員做的。他們在紙板跟報紙上挖出小洞，並把葵花子藏進洞裡。

content.en: It was a good day at the zoo. The chimps ate tasty sunflower seeds. The kids and zookeepers laughed a lot. They went home happy!
content.tw: 今天黑猩猩吃到了美味的葵花子，小朋友和管理員也開心歡笑，帶著愉快的心情回家。

content.en: But maybe the chimps thought about what kind of jobs they wanted! Hmm....
content.tw: 但說不定這些黑猩猩正在思考牠們想要什麼樣的工作呢！嗯...

vocabulary.preface: 黑猩猩不是真的在閱讀，而是在找美味的葵花子。

content.text:
1. job 工作。
I wonder if being a zookeeper is a good job. 不知道動物園管理員是不是好工作。
Did you just watch “We Bought a Zoo?” 你是不是剛剛看了[我們買了動物園]?
Good guess! 猜得真對!

2. find 覺得。
But that movie didn’t have any chimpanzees. 不過那部電影裡沒有黑猩猩。
I just love chimps! 我就愛黑猩猩!
I can’t believe you find them cute. 難以置信你覺得黑猩猩可愛。

3. laugh at 嘲笑。
They are. They even laugh at each other. 是很可愛，牠們甚至會互相嘲笑。

4. maybe 也許。
How can you tell? 你怎麼知道?
Because they laugh at me too! 因為牠們也會笑我!
Or maybe they’re just happy to see you. 也許牠們只是高興看到你。

vocabulary.postscript:
你想要什麼樣的工作?
job工作 find覺得 laugh at嘲笑 maybe也許

quiz.question: 1. What do job ads tell people about?
options: b. How to become a zookeeper

quiz.question: 2. What did zookeepers put in the holes?
3. What did the chimps do that day?

content.en: The Kites of India
content.tw: 印度的風箏

content.en: In India, people fly kites. They have been flying kites for hundreds of years. At festivals, thousands of colorful kites fly. Some people fly them from the tops of buildings! Kites high in the sky are beautiful!
content.tw: 印度人喜歡放風箏，這項活動已經有好幾百年的歷史。每到節慶的時候，就能看到成千上萬個彩色風箏在空中飛揚，非常美麗！

content.en: Flying kites is fun. But every year, fewer and fewer people fly them. Why?
content.tw: 放風箏很有趣，但為什麼放風箏的人每年都越來越少？

content.en: India is getting hotter. People like to stay inside. They use their phones. Kids play video games. They watch TV.
content.tw: 其中一個原因是，印度的天氣越來越熱。比起在戶外放風箏，很多人寧願待在室內，邊吹電扇邊滑手機、看電視。

content.en: Kites can be dangerous too. Some people like kite fighting. They try to cut another kite's string. One kite falls. The other kite wins!
content.tw: 另外，放風箏有時後也有危險。有些人喜歡「鬥風箏」。他們會想辦法割斷別人的風箏線。只要讓對方的風箏掉下來，玩家就獲勝了！

content.en: These kites have strong strings. Some have glass or metal on them. They are very sharp! They are good at cutting strings.
content.tw: 有些人會在風箏線黏上玻璃或是金屬，讓線變得更鋒利、攻擊力更強。

content.en: The kite strings fall with the kites. They can cut people too. Some people get bad cuts. And every year, these kite strings kill a few people…
content.tw: 但是當風箏掉下來，這些鋒利的線可能會割傷人。每年都有人因此喪命。

content.en: But some people have not stopped flying kites. Kites are part of India's story. They hope more people will fly kites!
content.tw: 儘管如此，還是有人喜愛風箏。它是印度文化重要的一部分，他們希望未來有更多人放風箏。

content.en: Do you like kites? Taiwan has a kite festival too! Go fly a kite there!
content.tw: 你喜歡風箏嗎？台灣也有風箏節喔！去那裡放風箏吧！

vocabulary.preface: 印度放風箏的人變少了，喜歡這項活動的人想找出原因。

content.text:
1. fewer and fewer 越來越少。
It seems there are fewer and fewer butterflies every year. 蝴蝶好像每年都在變少。
You are right! 你說得對! When I was little, 我小時候， I saw many butterflies in our backyard every day. 每天都在我家後院看到好多蝴蝶。

2. colorful 彩色的。
Me too, especially in the summertime. 我也是，特別在夏天。
Yeah, big and colorful butterflies everywhere. 對啊，到處都是又大又繽紛的蝴蝶。

3. hotter 更熱。
Now I’m lucky if I see a few. 現在看到幾隻就算幸運了。
I think it's because the weather keeps getting hotter. 我想是因為天氣一直變熱。

4. dangerous 危險的。
Heat waves can be dangerous for them. 熱浪對牠們可能造成危險。

vocabulary.postscript: 來一起讀單字。fewer and fewer越來越少 colorful彩色的 hotter更熱的 dangerous危險的

quiz.question: 1. How long have people in India been flying kites?
2. Why do people like to stay inside now?
3. What is trying to cut another kite's string called?

content.en: Every year, Taiwan has many Hungry Ghost festivals. It’s a very big, very old festival. Some say it’s more than two thousand years old! People honor the ghosts of family and friends who died.
content.tw: 台灣很多地方都會過中元節。 它大概有超過兩千多年的歷史。每年的中元節，大家會緬懷過世的親人。

content.en: People don’t want these ghosts to be hungry. During the year, people give them the foods they love.
content.tw: 為了不讓那些亡靈餓肚子，親人都會定期祭拜。

content.en: There are other ghosts. Maybe they have no family or friends. So nobody gives them food during the year. They are called "Hungry Ghosts." The festival is for them too.
content.tw: 但有一些亡靈在世上沒有親人，一年中沒有人給他們食物，只能挨餓 ，被稱為「餓鬼」。中元普渡這個節日也是為他們而設的。

content.en: This year, the festival started later than usual. So the ghosts are hungrier than usual! It’s a very, very Hungry Ghost Festival!
content.tw: 今年的中元節來得比較晚，孤魂野鬼特別餓!

content.en: Taiwan’s biggest Hungry Ghost Festival is in Keelung. People make lots of food for all ghosts. They also give the ghosts other things to make them happy.
content.tw: 台灣最盛大的中元節祭典在基隆。為了讓鬼魂高興，人們會準備各種食物和禮物。

content.en: When it’s time for the ghosts to go home, people light lanterns. People take the lanterns to the ocean and put them on the water. The lanterns help the ghosts go home.
content.tw: 祭典結束時，還會在港口邊放水燈，照亮好兄弟回家的路！

content.en: This year, you can also honor the ghosts during the Hungry Ghost Festival.
content.tw: 今年，你也可以在中元節祭拜好兄弟。

vocabulary.preface: 中元節是重要的民俗節日，今年落在九月六號。

content.text:
1. die 死亡。
I’m so hungry I could die.我快餓死了。
Careful, if you die, you’ll come back as a ghost. 小心哦，你假如真的死了，會變成鬼回來。
At least ghosts don’t get hungry. 至少做鬼不挨餓。

2. hungry 飢餓的。
From what I heard, they do. 我聽到的是他們也會餓。That’s why we have the Hungry Ghost Festival. 這是為什麼我們有餓鬼節。
That’s the English name.那是英文名字。

3. food 食物。
It’s called the Zhongyuan Festival in Chinese. 中文叫做中元節。
But you offer food to the spirits that day, right? 但是那一天你會拿食物祭拜亡靈，對吧?

4. friend 朋友。
Yes. Rice, fruit and chicken.對，有米飯，水果，還有雞。 Ghosts have friends, too.鬼也是有朋友的。

vocabulary.postscript: 我們一起來讀單字。die死亡 hungry飢餓的 food食物 friend朋友

quiz.question: 1. How old do some say the Hungry Ghost Festival is?
2. Where is Taiwan’s biggest Hungry Ghost Festival?
3. What helps the Hungry Ghosts go home?

title: Fans Go to Temples for Concert Tickets

content.en: Do you like going to concerts? It is exciting to see your favorite singers!
content.tw: 你有去演唱會追星過嗎? 親眼看到喜歡的歌手會很開心!

content.en: But sometimes good tickets are hard to get. They sell fast! So some fans stay up late to get tickets online. Some learn to type fast. Then they can get tickets online fast!
content.tw: 熱門的票券經常很難搶，所以很多粉絲會想盡辦法弄到票。

content.en: But guess what more and more fans are doing? They are going to temples! Fans are going to temples for Yue Lao. He is famous. He helps people find love. Some fans say seeing their favorite singers is like trying to find love! They say Yue Lao can help them get good tickets!
content.tw: 也有人請神明來幫忙！粉絲會去月老廟祈求助一臂之力！他們相信既然能幫信徒找到愛情，也能幫他們搶到演唱會門票！

content.en: Many people give fruit and snacks at temples. Fans give these to Yue Lao too. But some also give him pictures or dolls of their favorite singers. Some give him food their favorite singers like. Fans also write to Yue Lao. They write the concert name and the singer's name. They ask for good tickets. They are doing something new with an old way!
content.tw: 很多人會把零食和水果作為供品，也會帶歌手的照片、娃娃、或歌手喜歡的食物，也在卡片上寫好演唱會跟歌手的資訊給月老看，以免他搞錯人！

content.en: Is there a singer you want to see? Can Yue Lao help?
content.tw: 有沒有你想見的歌手呢？月老能不能幫上忙？

vocabulary.preface: 大明星表演的門票經常秒殺，歌迷只好另外想辦法。

content.text:
1. ticket 門票。
You’ve been sitting there for an hour.你坐在那裏一個鐘頭了。Did you get the tickets or not? 究竟買到票沒有?
I don’t know.我不知道。

2. stay up 熬夜。
For 3 seconds I thought I did.有三秒鐘我以為買到了。But then it went back to circling.但是它又回去轉圈圈。
You already stayed up until 2AM last night! 你昨晚已經熬夜到兩點。

3. get 得到。
I know, and I really don’t want to do that tonight.我知道，今天晚上真的不想那樣了。
Then give it up. 那放棄吧。They are too hard to get.票太難買了。

4. fan 歌迷，或是影迷。
Give up? Never! 放棄?絕不! I’ve waited for them forever! 我等他們一輩子了!
Wow, you really are a true fan ! 哇，你還真的是忠實歌迷!

vocabulary.postscript: 今天的單字很常用。ticket門票 stay up熬夜 get得到 fan歌迷，或是影迷

quiz.question: 1. Where are more and more fans of singers going?
2. Who do fans ask for good tickets?
3. What do some fans give?
options: a. Pictures of their favorite singers

title: Boy Wins Taiwan’s First Skateboarding Medal

content.en: Taiwan has a new champion! He is a skateboarder. He won a silver medal! And he is eleven years old!
content.tw: 一名十一歲的台灣小男孩在亞洲錦標賽獲得了滑板銀牌！

content.en: When this boy was five, he started skiing. It was hard! Then his friend said skateboarding could help him ski better. He liked it. Soon he was skating every day!
content.tw: 男孩五歲開始學滑雪。滑雪不容易，有人告訴他，玩滑板可以幫助他的滑雪技巧。於是，他開始練習滑板，很快就愛上這項運動！

content.en: One day, the boy and his dad were skiing in Japan. His dad got a phone call. It was Taiwan's skateboarding team! They asked the boy to be on the team. Wow! What a surprise! The boy said yes!
content.tw: 有一天，男孩跟他的爸爸在日本滑雪，爸爸接到來自臺灣滑板代表隊一通電話，邀請男孩加入隊伍。男孩非常驚喜，立刻就答應了！

content.en: The team went to the Asian Skateboarding Championships. It was in China. The boy worked hard. But he did not think about winning a medal.
content.tw: 台灣代表隊前往中國參加亞洲滑板錦標賽。雖然男孩很努力，但是他對於拿獎牌並沒有想太多。

content.en: The boy skated. He fell! But he got up. He skated again. He fell again! And he got up again!
content.tw: 比賽時男孩卻摔了一跤，但是他立刻爬起來，之後又摔了一次，他也馬上站起來繼續比賽。

content.en: The boy skated one more time. This time, he made a great move! It was the first time he made it! Everyone cheered!
content.tw: 第三次嘗試時，他成功完成了一個精彩的高難度動作！全場觀眾都為他熱烈歡呼！

content.en: The boy won Taiwan's first skateboarding championship medal! Way to go, kid! Go, Team Taiwan!
content.tw: 這位男孩贏得了台灣第一面滑板錦標賽獎牌！做得好，孩子！台灣隊加油！

vocabulary.preface: 來自台灣的男孩，榮獲亞洲滑板錦標賽第二名。

content.text:
1. one more time 再一次。
Hurry up! 趕快! The game is about to start! 比賽就要開始了!
I know. I just need to tie my shoes one more time.我知道，我只是需要再綁一次鞋帶。

2. this time 這一次。
Believe me, your shoes look perfect.想信我，你的鞋子很完美了。
There, this time they are really perfect.好了，這次它真的完美了。

3. gold medal 金牌。
Now, we are playing against the Shark team, right? 現在我們的對手是鯊魚隊，對吧?
Yeah, and if we win, we’ll get the gold medal.對，假如我們贏就拿金牌。

4. start 開始。
What do you plan to do with the medal? 你準備拿那個牌子做什麼?
I’ll start calling myself “Champion”! 我會開始叫自己[冠軍]!

vocabulary.postscript: 你喜歡體育競賽嗎? one more time再一次 this time這一次 gold medal金牌 start開始

quiz.question: 1. How old is the boy?
2. Where were the boy and his dad skiing?
3. Where did the team go?

title: A Big Beautiful Balloon in a Backyard

content.en: Hot air balloons are very big balloons. They go high in the sky. A big basket is under each balloon. A pilot and riders are in it.
content.tw: 熱氣球非常巨大，可以飛到高空。熱氣球下方有一個大籃子，搭載飛行員跟乘客。

content.en: These balloons need fuel to make hot air. Hot air goes up. With hot air inside, the balloons go up! They go high, above trees and houses! Well, they do most of the time!
content.tw: 熱氣球需要燃料來製造熱空氣。有了熱空氣，熱氣球就能升空，飛越樹木和房屋，往高空而去。至少計畫是那樣。

content.en: One day, a man was watching TV. His wife was doing yoga. It was a normal day. Then someone knocked on their door. He said a big balloon was in their backyard! How weird!
content.tw: 這一天跟平常一樣，一名男子正在家看電視，太太則在做瑜伽。突然有人來敲門說，熱氣球落在他們家後院！

content.en: The couple went to their backyard. They saw the hot air balloon! It was beautiful. It was also very big. Their backyard was very small! The balloon pilot was very good. The balloon did not hit the house. It did not hit the trees!
content.tw: 這對夫妻趕緊跑到後院，果然看到一顆漂亮的巨大熱氣球！儘管後院很小，技術超高的飛行員倒是沒有撞到房子或是樹木。

content.en: Why did the balloon need to land? The fuel was low. The wind also changed. The pilot needed to land fast!
content.tw: 原來，熱氣球的燃料快用完了，風向也突然改變。飛行員需要立刻尋找安全的地方降落，所以只好降落在他們的後院！

content.en: Twelve riders were in the balloon basket. They were scared. But the pilot landed safely!
content.tw: 搭乘熱氣球的12名乘客都嚇壞了! 但至少飛行員讓它安全降落，大家都平安無事！

content.en: What a weird day!
content.tw: 多麼奇怪的一天！

vocabulary.preface: 熱氣球忽然降落在自己家後院，這可稀奇了!

content.text:
1. pilot 飛行員。
Did you feel that? 你有沒有感覺到? Why is our plane going up and down?
我們的飛機為什麼在上上下下?
Maybe the pilot is trying something new? 也許飛行員在嘗試新動作?

2. in the sky 在天上。
Trying it when we’re high in the sky? 在高空中嘗試? This is not normal! 這很不尋常!

3. weird 奇怪的。
It is a little weird, isn’t it? 是有點奇怪。But relax. We are about to land. Only some 15 minutes away. 不過放輕鬆，我們再15分鐘就要降落了。

4. land 降落。
See, we landed safely. 你看，我們安全落地了。

vocabulary.postscript:
Whew! For a few seconds I was really scared! 有幾秒鐘的時間我好害怕!
搭熱氣球很好玩喔，來讀單字。pilot飛行員 in the sky在天上 weird奇怪的 land降落

quiz.question: 1. What makes hot air balloons go up?
2. What was the man doing?
3. Why did the balloon need to land?
